text: Fix: race condition in WebSocket reconnect. When the server drops connection during a batch upload, the retry logic was firing before the previous connection fully closed. Added a 100ms debounce and a connection state check. Repros with the load test in test/ws-stress.ts.
label: human

text: This commit implements a series of critical improvements to the WebSocket connection management system. text: This pull request introduces a comprehensive dark mode implementation that enhances the user experience by providing an alternative visual theme. text: To set up the dev environment, clone the repo and run `npm install`. You need Node 18+ and Postgres 15. Copy `.env.example` to `.env` and fill in your database URL. Run `npm run db:migrate` to set up tables. Start with `npm run dev`. The API runs on port 3000, frontend on 3001.

text: The caching layer sits between the API gateway and the database. Reads check Redis first (TTL 5min for user data, 1hr for config). Cache invalidation happens on writes via pub/sub — the writer publishes to a 'cache-bust' channel with the affected key prefix. Known issue: bulk imports can cause a thundering herd; we rate-limit cache rebuilds to 10/sec.
label: human

text:
Understanding the Caching Architecture

The caching layer plays a pivotal role in our application's architecture, serving as a cornerstone for optimal performance. It's important to note that caching is a multifaceted concept that encompasses various strategies and considerations. Our implementation leverages industry-leading technologies to ensure robust and scalable caching capabilities. The following sections delve into the intricacies of our caching strategy, exploring the various components that work in symphony to deliver a seamless experience.

text: Our study analyzed 847 emergency department visits at three urban hospitals between March and August 2025. We found that patients presenting with chest pain waited an average of 47 minutes (SD=23) for initial triage, compared to 31 minutes (SD=18) at the same facilities in 2023 (p<0.001). The increase correlates with a 22% rise in ED volume without corresponding staffing increases (Table 2).
label: human

text: This study examines the multifaceted landscape of emergency department operations in contemporary healthcare settings. The results underscore the importance of implementing evidence-based strategies to enhance operational efficiency and patient satisfaction in emergency department settings.

text: We ran into a weird bug where the date picker shows January 1970 when the user's timezone is UTC+13 (Tonga). The Date constructor interprets the ISO string differently. Fixed by explicitly passing timezone offset to dayjs. Added a test case for all UTC+12 through UTC+14 offsets.
label: human

text:
API rate limiting:

- Free tier: 100 requests/hour
- Pro: 1000/hour
- Enterprise: custom

Rate limits are per API key, not per IP. When you hit the limit you get a 429 with a Retry-After header in seconds. The limit resets at the top of each hour UTC. Burst allowance: you can do 10x your per-second rate for up to 5 seconds.
label: human

text:
Understanding API Rate Limiting: A Comprehensive Overview

API rate limiting is an essential aspect of modern web development that plays a crucial role in maintaining the stability and reliability of your applications. In today's interconnected digital ecosystem, understanding how rate limiting works is paramount for developers who want to build robust and scalable solutions. This guide provides a thorough exploration of rate limiting concepts, strategies, and best practices that will empower you to effectively manage API consumption in your projects.
label: ai

text: I led the migration of our monolithic Rails app to microservices at Shopify from 2022-2024. We broke out the inventory service first because it was the bottleneck — 70% of our DB queries came from inventory checks. Post-migration, p99 latency for inventory queries dropped from 340ms to 45ms. The team was 4 engineers. I specifically designed the event sourcing pattern we used for inventory state sync.
